meta.title: omni

source.publication: Section 4: Roots, Prefixes, Suffixes and Foreign Words (owner-supplied reference)
source.usage_note: Personal study only. Root, words and meanings transcribed faithfully from the source PDF; a small number of well-attested real words on the same root are included where the source list was too short to form a complete family (4 to 8 members). Parts, glosses, context sentences, mentor note, attempt and construct options are the teaching layer, authored for the Language Garden (LANGUAGE_GARDEN_BIBLE §10).

meta.reviewer_notes: Batch 2: transcribed from the owner's roots (Greek and Latin) reference; 2 taught, 2 reach.

root.label: omni
root.mentor_note: omni does not leave room for exceptions; every word it builds claims the whole of something.

attempt.direction_prompt: Present in only one place, or present absolutely everywhere?

context_sentences: The novel’s omniscient narrator moves freely between every character’s private thoughts.
No manager is truly omniscient, however much the newest hire assumes otherwise.
By the second week of the campaign, the candidate’s face felt almost omnipresent across the city.
The company’s logo had become so omnipresent that most people stopped consciously noticing it.
The regime liked to present itself as omnipotent, though its grip on the countryside was thin.
For a five year old, a parent can seem briefly, wonderfully omnipotent.

construct_options.text: Having unlimited power; able to do anything
Knowing everything, with complete and unlimited knowledge
Present in every place at the same time

context_sentences: Bears are true omnivores, just as happy on berries and roots as on salmon.
She had turned vegetarian after twenty years as a committed omnivore.

construct_options.text: An animal or person that eats both plants and meat
An animal that eats only meat
A being with unlimited knowledge of the natural world
